title: Fluent Bit Storage Backlog File Count and Age
app: Fluent Bit storage metrics HTTP endpoint
spl:
index=fluent sourcetype="fluentbit:storage"
| eval chunks_up=coalesce(chunks_up, up_chunks, storage_chunks_up)
| eval max_age_sec=coalesce(chunk_up_age_sec, oldest_chunk_age_sec)
| where chunks_up > 50 OR max_age_sec > 600
| stats latest(chunks_up) as up, latest(max_age_sec) as age by host, input_name, output_name
| sort -age
description: Persistent chunk files that do not drain indicate the output is slower than ingress for extended periods—often TLS issues, HEC 503s, or disk IO limits on IoT gateways. Count and age together separate brief spikes from stuck backlogs.
value: Prevents storage exhaustion on edge devices and highlights regional Splunk indexer saturation.
implementation: Align `input_name`/`output_name` with Fluent Bit config. Join to host disk free metrics. Alert when `age` exceeds RPO targets for that log class.
visualization: Dual-axis (chunks × age), map of edge sites, table sorted by age.
grandmaExplanation: We read the forwarder and buffer logs for stress and give-ups so we see log loss risk before disks or patience run out.
knownFalsePositives: Buffer pain from downstream saturation, bad TLS, or local disk. We check agent and destination before blind buffer tuning.
